key_insight: Off-the-shelf multimodal LLMs can zero-shot identify stress-critical regions from physics-guided prompts and act as semantic proxies for 3D mesh refinement.
engineering_relevance: This could reduce reliance on expensive PDE-driven adaptivity or large supervised surrogate models, making simulation workflows faster and more accessible.
method: GReFEM uses multimodal LLMs to localize refinement regions from textual physics prompts and geometry images.
An orthoViews module selects 2D views that best expose 3D geometric features to the model.
The approach is benchmarked against a tuned geometric heuristic under the same refinement budget.
evidence_signal: The paper reports evaluation across diverse CAD geometries, loading cases, and state-of-the-art MLLMs, with results showing higher precision than blind heuristics in locating stress-relevant features.
watch_item: Performance depends on view selection and 2D visual grounding, so complex 3D geometries or poorly chosen views may still limit reliability.
paper_title: GReFEM: Multimodal LLMs as Zero-Shot Semantic Assistants for Physics-Guided 3D Mesh Refinement
